behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real `git` subprocess + rename detection/output format (e.g., rename status records and scores). A fake parser input can miss real CLI/subprocess formatting and index/worktree interactions, so MOCK-HIDE applies. REJECTION-GATE: not Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is purely git change categorization, with no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI, and assert report has rename count/file entry while add/delete do not include that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real git diff behavior for binary content and real subprocess/stdout decoding. In-process stubs can hide byte/format edge cases, so REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE push to System. REJECTION-GATE: no AI/ML output quality dependency in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file, run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary file is represented in counts/output.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure is tied to real unborn-HEAD git behavior (exit codes/messages from subprocess). A mock may not reproduce exact no-commit repo semantics, so Integration is too low under REAL-INFRA. REJECTION-GATE: behavior text is about git repo state handling, not model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert successful exit and empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real repository history, requiring real git history traversal via subprocess. Stubs can validate arithmetic but can hide history/query mismatches, so System is minimum. REJECTION-GATE: no AI/ML call or multi-agent chain in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with added/deleted/renamed/modified files across commits, run the CLI, and compare reported counts to independently computed expected counts from git commands.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real git index vs working-tree semantics, which are OS/subprocess-backed and easy to misrepresent with fakes. TIE-BREAKER evidence for higher level: behavior explicitly contrasts "staged or unstaged," a real git state boundary. REJECTION-GATE: no AI/ML-dependent correctness.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create the same file change in two setups (staged-only and unstaged-only), run CLI for each, and assert identical structured output.
